title: Index Ventures 合伙人 Achadjian：AI 下一前沿是物理世界，SpaceX IPO 意义重大
full_text:
Jun 5th, 2026
Index Ventures' Achadjian on Backing Ex-SpaceX Workers
Software has dominated venture capital for years, but as AI moves beyond the digital world and into manufacturing, robotics, and industrial systems, investors are betting the next trillion-dollar opportunities wraps software into the physical world. Nina Achadjian, partner at Index Ventures, an early backer of companies including Anthropic, Physical Intelligence, and ServiceTitan, discusses the next frontier in AI and the SpaceX IPO's significance on the venture space. She joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech"
Duration: 10:14
Strong Jobs Report Boosts Bets on Fed Rate Hike
Duration: 1:20
Nvidia Clears ‘Big Three’ for Vera Rubin HBM4 Supply
Duration: 3:53
Fed Is in No Hurry to Raise Rates, BlackRock's Rosenberg Says
Duration: 2:54
Sports City: The World Cup Keeps Getting Bigger
Duration: 3:13
Rick Rieder Likens US Economy to 'Three-Month-Old Birthday Cake'
Duration: 1:59
SpaceX IPO Denied Fast Track Entry by S&P
Duration: 1:56
Semiconductor Stocks Got 'Over Their Skis,' Strategist Bartels Says
Duration: 1:41
Inflation Doesn’t Need a Big Response, Economist Roth Says
